Items.Key: command injection via system() with user input in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|line 605-608: `if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }` — user-supplied command after 'exec ' prefix is passed directly to system() without sanitization after authentication check on line 603.|remote code execution with the privileges of the running process. an attacker can execute arbitrary os commands after authenticating.|user input via 'admin exec <command>' after successful authentication|remove system() call entirely. if command execution is required, use a whitelist of allowed commands with strict parameter validation, or use execve() with explicit argument arrays.

truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 9 of 20 true vulnerabilities. Found full coverage for command injection (2 vectors), format string, buffer overflows (2), use-after-free, hardcoded credentials, TOCTOU, weak PRNG, and integer division by zero. Missed 11 vulnerabilities including integer overflow in factorial, race conditions, logic bomb, path traversal, thread-unsafe timestamp cache, insecure temp files, persistent auth state, unhandled exceptions, integer underflow, unbounded recursion, and data race in result cache. Multiple severity misclassifications: format string and use-after-free rated High instead of Critical; hardcoded credentials and weak PRNG rated Medium instead of High; division by zero rated Medium instead of Low; heap overflow misclassified as CWE-120 instead of CWE-122.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 237-238: `std::string active_format = runtime_log_format(); printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);` — The format string is loaded from config (line 556: `string_utils::set_runtime_log_format(log_format->second);`) and can be set by an attacker via config file.
Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified the format string vulnerability with exact code quote matching source lines 237-238. The vulnerability exists: runtime_log_format() returns a string settable via config, and printf uses it as format string with user_input as argument.
Run 2 did not identify integer overflow in factorial computation. Source lines 316-318 show `result *= i;` with long long that can overflow for large inputs without bounds checking.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 817, 821-826: `char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); ... thread_pool_->enqueue([scratch, copy_len] { ... volatile unsigned int checksum = 0; for (size_t i = 0; i < copy_len; ++i) checksum += static_cast<unsigned char>(scratch[i]); ... });` Lines 883-884: `stage_deferred_history_flush(); memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup();`
Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified use-after-free with exact quotes from source. stage_deferred_history_flush allocates scratch memory and enqueues tasks, then cleanup() frees the pool before tasks complete.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 564-567: `std::string validator_cmd = get_setting("validator_path", "/usr/bin/validator"); std::string config_file = get_setting("config_file", "default.conf"); std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());`
Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified command injection in config validation with exact code quote matching source lines 564-567.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 831-834: `if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }`
Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified stack buffer overflow with exact code quote matching source lines 831-834. The conditional uses strcpy when expression length >= buffer size.

Run 2 did not identify race condition in ThreadSafeCounter. Source lines 478-487 show volatile long long with non-atomic read-modify-write: `long long old_value = value_;` then `value_ = old_value + 1;` inside lock_guard, but the read occurs outside the lock.
Run 2 did not identify logic bomb. Source lines 592-598 show that after 5 failed login attempts, EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE string in input sets authenticated_=true without proper verification.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal. Source line 543: `std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;` allows arbitrary filename input without sanitization, enabling directory traversal.

Previous answer correctly identified heap buffer overflow with exact code quotes. expression_buffer_ is 256 bytes but strcpy has no bounds check. Note: previous answer classified as CWE-120 (stack) but this is heap (CWE-122).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 73: `constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";` — Admin password is hardcoded as a compile-time constant in source code.
Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified hardcoded credentials with exact code quote matching source line 73.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 635-639: `std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); return "TOKEN_" + std::to_string(dis(gen));` — Session token is only 4 random digits (1000-9999), providing only ~13 bits of entropy.
Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified weak PRNG for session tokens with exact code quote matching source lines 635-639.

Run 2 did not identify thread-unsafe timestamp cache. Source lines 230-231: `static std::tm cached_time; if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;` — localtime is not thread-safe and cached_time is static.
Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling. Source lines 624-632 use TEMP/TMPDIR environment variables with system() command for emergency log, vulnerable to injection.
Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state. Source lines 579-582, 584-599 show authenticated_, login_attempts_, session_token_ persist across calls without proper session management.
Run 2 did not identify unhandled exception. Source line 729: `double value = std::stod(tokens[3]);` in handle_variable_command can throw if tokens[3] is not a valid number.
Run 2 did not identify integer underflow. Source lines 132-136: `size_t remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES;` — if previous_size is small, this underflows (size_t is unsigned).

Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion. Source lines 381-433 show parse_expression calling parse_term calling parse_factor which calls parse_expression on PAREN_OPEN, with no depth limit despite MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH constant.

Run 2 did not identify data race in result cache. Source lines 440-451 show result_cache_ accessed without cache_mutex_ in evaluate_cached, and line 808 shows evaluate_cached called from thread_pool_->enqueue without synchronization.
Previous answer correctly identified TOCTOU with exact code quote matching source lines 544-546. The 25ms sleep between exists check and file open enables race condition.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 750-752: `size_t token_end = input.find_first_of(" \t"); size_t token_length = (token_end == std::string::npos) ? input.length() : token_end; return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);`
Items.Notes: Previous answer correctly identified integer division by zero with exact code quote matching source lines 750-752. Empty input produces token_length=0 causing division by zero.
